summary: Install-time delegation to sibling @doaction/shared with telemetry purpose
details: Package ships a thin preinstall wrapper that delegates entirely to a sibling dependency. package.json declares "preinstall": "node scripts/postinstall.js", and that script's only logic is `require('@doaction/shared/bin/postinstall.js')` inside a try/catch that swallows non-MODULE_NOT_FOUND errors. The package itself contains no network I/O, secret reads, or obfuscation — its only install-time effect is to load and execute @doaction/shared at install time. The package self-describes as 'Examples collection with Datadog environment telemetry for internal testing', is published at the placeholder version 9.9.9, and pins @doaction/shared at ^9.9.9. The runtime export reportExamplesEnv calls collectEnv/sendToDatadog from the sibling. Installer-side risk is fully transitive on @doaction/shared; route to human review so that sibling package can be assessed in conjunction with this wrapper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
